title: Authenticate to cloud via OIDC, not stored keys
description: Exchange the workflow's OIDC token for short-lived cloud credentials instead of storing long-lived access keys as secrets.
body: Use when a workflow deploys to or reads from a cloud provider. Do not store static AWS/GCP/Azure access keys as repo secrets when OIDC federation is available, since static keys leak and never expire. Actions: set permissions: id-token: write, configure a cloud role trust policy scoped to the repo and ref, and use the provider's configure-credentials action to assume the role; constrain the trust policy by branch/environment. Validation signals include no long-lived cloud key secrets present and a trust policy bound to the exact repo and ref. Risk boundary: a broad OIDC trust policy (any repo, any ref) or fallback to stored keys is a privilege finding needing review.
